dropped.reason: Warm brown bleed from a hero/testimonial background photo — not a brand color.
Desaturated sibling of navy — collapses into the primary navy hierarchy.

retained.evidence: Featured in logo plate, 'START YOUR FREE TRIAL' ribbon, and all primary CTA buttons.
Section background for testimonials and class grid.

contrastIssues: #FFEE03 vs. #EEEEEE (current yellow-on-light-gray CTAs) = 1.3:1 — fails WCAG AA dramatically.
#223855 vs. #EEEEEE for headings passes (9.3:1) but loses visual punch against the lifeless gray.
Current site mixes dark-hero and light-body inconsistently, fragmenting the brand.
colorAudit.harmonyType: complementary-athletic (electric yellow + deep navy — a naturally sportswear-coded pairing)
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: weak — the electric yellow competes with stock hero imagery instead of anchoring the brand. No clear surface/background hierarchy.
colorAudit.saturationNotes: Yellow is at the absolute edge of saturation/brightness — it needs a dark substrate to feel premium instead of cheap.

modeRationale: The existing site already uses a dark hero as its strongest moment — we commit to dark across all sections. Yellow #FFEE03 is only visually premium against a deep substrate; on white it reads as a 1995 highlighter. Dark substrate also resolves the current site's light/dark inconsistency and lets the performance-athletic-skill kinetic typography land.
colorShifts: Kept #FFEE03 verbatim as --accent — it's the strongest brand asset and the whole design will orbit it.
Kept #223855 as --secondary-deep for section dividers, chart bars, and team-card badges.
Shifted --bg from #EEEEEE (light gray) to #0B0C0E (near-black with a cold tint) — lets the yellow hit like a signal flare.
Introduced two dark surface tiers (#141518 card, #1B1D21 elevated) so bento/stat cards have hierarchy without losing the blackout feeling.
Derived --border from the accent at 8% opacity so structure reads as 'on-brand' even without yellow fill.
Dropped #463939 and #587291 — neither survived logo/navbar validation.

fontRecommendation.display: Archivo Black (900) OR Syne 700-800 — wide, athletic, sportswear-coded
fontRecommendation.mono: JetBrains Mono 500 (for technique/time labels, schedule grids)
fontRecommendation.reasoning: Performance-athletic-skill calls for wide, brand-forward display type paired with neutral, highly legible body. Archivo Black delivers the 'stamped on a jersey' energy that matches 10P's competitive identity. Inter body keeps technique descriptions and schedules airtight.
